faqs.question: Why choose 0-100kPa over 0-400kPa for air applications?
faqs.answer: The 0-100kPa range provides better resolution for low-pressure air applications: 1) 4x better resolution - 100kPa/10000 counts = 0.01kPa (0.1 mbar) vs 0.04kPa; 2) Better accuracy at low pressures - same ±0.5% FS means ±0.5kPa error vs ±2kPa; 3) Better signal-to-noise ratio for small pressure changes; 4) Typical HVAC air pressures are 0.5-5kPa - using only 1-5% of 0-400kPa range wastes resolution. For example, measuring 2kPa duct pressure: 0-100kPa uses 2% of range with good resolution; 0-400kPa uses only 0.5% with poor resolution. The 0-100kPa transmitter provides more precise measurement and control for air systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 0-100kPa for air pressure <50kPa. Use 0-400kPa for water or higher-pressure applications.

faqs.question: What clean room applications is this suitable for?
faqs.answer: The MSM-P100-010 is suitable for various clean room pressure monitoring: 1) Room differential pressure - monitoring positive/negative pressure vs adjacent spaces; 2) Filter monitoring - measuring pressure drop across HEPA filters; 3) Airflow measurement - converting velocity pressure to airflow rate; 4) Fume hood face velocity - monitoring static pressure for safety; 5) Isolation room pressure - ensuring proper containment. The ±0.5% accuracy ensures reliable pressure differentials for contamination control. The stainless steel construction meets clean room material requirements. The 0-10V output integrates with clean room monitoring systems. Typical clean room differentials are 10-50 Pa (0.01-0.05kPa) - the transmitter's resolution can detect these small pressures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for all clean room pressure monitoring. Resolution adequate for typical 10-50 Pa differentials.

faqs.question: How do I measure differential pressure with this transmitter?
faqs.answer: To measure differential pressure with the MSM-P100-010: 1) Use two transmitters - one upstream, one downstream; 2) Connect both to BMS and calculate differential in software; 3) Or use a differential pressure transmitter with dual ports. For filter monitoring: 1) Mount one transmitter upstream of filter; 2) Mount second transmitter downstream; 3) Differential = Upstream - Downstream; 4) Set alarm when differential exceeds limit (indicating dirty filter). For room differential: 1) One transmitter in clean room; 2) One in adjacent area; 3) Monitor difference for proper pressure cascade. The 0-100kPa range is suitable for most HVAC differential pressures (typically <10kPa). For very low differentials (<1kPa), the resolution is adequate for reliable measurement.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use two transmitters for differential measurement. Calculate differential in BMS or use dedicated differential transmitter.

faqs.question: Can this transmitter be used for water applications?
faqs.answer: The MSM-P100-010 can be used for low-pressure water applications, but with limitations: 1) 0-100kPa = 0-10m water column - suitable for very low water pressures only; 2) Most water applications (pumps, hydronics) require 0-400kPa or higher; 3) The transmitter is compatible with water - stainless steel construction resists corrosion. For water applications: 1) Verify pressure range is adequate (add 50% safety margin); 2) Install with isolation valve for maintenance; 3) Mount below pipe to avoid air pockets; 4) Use for: open tank level (10m max), very low-pressure water systems, drip irrigation. For typical HVAC water systems, use MSM-P400-010 or MSM-P400-420 instead. The 0-100kPa range is primarily designed for air and very low-pressure fluids.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for water only if pressure <50kPa. For typical water applications, select higher-range models.

faqs.question: What is the minimum measurable pressure?
faqs.answer: The MSM-P100-010 can reliably measure pressures down to approximately 0.1kPa (100 Pa, 0.4 inH2O): 1) Theoretical resolution: 100kPa / 10000 ADC counts = 0.01kPa per count; 2) Practical minimum: ~10 counts = 0.1kPa with reasonable accuracy; 3) Below 0.1kPa, noise and offset errors become significant; 4) Zero offset drift may affect very low measurements. For reference: 1) 0.1kPa ≈ 0.4 inches water column; 2) Typical duct velocity pressure: 0.1-0.5kPa; 3) Clean room differential: 0.01-0.05kPa (may be at limit). For pressures below 0.1kPa, consider specialized low-pressure transmitters or differential pressure sensors designed for very low ranges. The transmitter can indicate pressures below 0.1kPa but accuracy is not guaranteed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Reliable measurement down to 0.1kPa. Below this, accuracy degrades. Consider specialized sensors for very low pressures.
